intro: Below are some of the resources that have genuinely helped me throughout my journey. I will continue adding more resources over time that I believe may also benefit others.

resources.title: Python for Materials Science
resources.description: A GitHub repository containing tutorials on Python utilities relevant to Materials Science, covering topics from basic concepts to advanced libraries in the field. The content is presented in the way I have learned and applied it throughout my work. Feedback and suggestions for improvement are always welcome.

resources.title: Machine Learning
resources.description: Below are resources for learning Machine Learning as a beginner. The first section contains foundational resources for learning ML from a general perspective, while the second builds on applications in materials science.

items.label: Stanford CS 229: Machine Learning
items.description: Comprehensive lecture series taught by Prof. Andrew Ng.

items.label: University of Waterloo SYDE 522: Machine Intelligence
items.description: Winter 2019 curriculum taught by Professor H.R. Tizhoosh.

items.label: Imperial College London MATE70026: Machine Learning For Materials
items.description: Specialized academic course taught by Prof. Aron Walsh.

items.label: Machine Learning in Materials Science (Reference Book)
items.description: Comprehensive textbook by F. Oviedo, K. T. Butler, and P. Canepa (Wiley-VCH, 2024).

items.label: Introduction to Materials Informatics
items.description: Digital interactive book by Prof. Enze Chen and Prof. Mark Asta.

items.label: Materials Informatics Lecture Series
items.description: Video playlist taught by Prof. Taylor Sparks.

resources.title: Scientific Slide Design
resources.description: The resources below are for designing high-quality figures and presentations for clearer science communication, covering the choice of colours, design elements, and slide layouts.

items.label: Make Attractive Scientific Figures in PowerPoint
items.description: Video tutorial on color, typography, and element positioning by Dr. Anubhav Jain (Atom to Materials channel).

items.label: Ram Seshadri's Slide Graphics Thread
items.description: Outstanding, detailed guidelines on preparing clear, high-quality figures and graphics for presentations and publications.

items.label: Coolors Palette Generator
items.description: A powerful, interactive utility for generating clean, harmonious color palettes and gradients.

items.label: WebAIM Color Contrast Checker
items.description: Essential tool for verifying text-to-background contrast ratios against web accessibility standards.

items.label: Making Scientific Figures with Illustrator and Blender
items.description: Comprehensive tutorial series on scientific illustration and 3D modeling by Christopher Gutierrez (UCLA).

resources.note: * Suggested by Dr. Arijit Sinha — I am deeply grateful for his excellent guidance.
